choices: Inconsistent class names splitting one category into two training signals
items.explain: Dog versus puppy strings fracture a single pet class without guideline collapse.

items.prompt: Which agreement measure is described for two raters on nominal labels?
choices: Model F1 on a test split
items.explain: Cohen's kappa is the pairwise chance-corrected measure in Table 4.4.

items.prompt: In Example 4.32, precision and recall on a gold audit set evaluate the annotator, not the trained detector.
items.explain: Labeler audit metrics answer a different question than model test metrics.

items.prompt: What should a team do when a pilot reaches kappa near 0.35 because guidelines are unclear, as in Example 4.31?
items.rubric: Revise guidelines/examples, retrain, adjudicate—do not scale up blindly.
items.sample_answer: Revise the guideline with clearer examples, retrain annotators, and rerun the pilot before full-corpus labeling.

items.prompt: Which is NOT listed as a frequent annotation failure mode in this clip?
choices: Boundary slack in boxes or masks
items.explain: Learning-rate tuning is model training, not a label QC failure mode.
